op-reverb-rebate.h: A Hard Rock Hotel on Pensacola's Waterfront Is Worth Building. Not at $58 Million in Public Money.
op-reverb-rebate.d: Dawson Company wants to put a Reverb by Hard Rock and a 15-story apartment tower on a downtown parcel that has sat empty for 14 years. The city was right to reject the developer's tax rebate request last week. Here's what a defensible deal would look

alice-williams-capc.h: Pensacola Council Advances Sale of Former Alice S. Williams Day Care Building to CAPC
alice-williams-capc.d: The city will transfer the 1015 N. E Street property to Community Action Program Committee, the nonprofit that runs Head Start in Escambia and Santa Rosa counties. The council held the public hearing and declared the building surplus at its April 23

council-cardboard-ordinance.h: Pensacola Repeals Its Separate Corrugated Cardboard Ordinance
council-cardboard-ordinance.d: The City Council approved Ordinance 05-26 on April 23, removing Section 4-3-66 of the city code. Commercial cardboard handling now falls under the city's broader solid waste rules.

council-opioid-settlement.h: Pensacola Joins Multi-State Opioid Settlement With Six Remaining Defendants
council-opioid-settlement.d: The City Council approved participation in a settlement agreement that adds Pensacola to the national opioid litigation pool targeting a final group of manufacturers and distributors. The settlement returns money to the city for opioid remediation pr

legion-field-gla-sidewalks.h: Pensacola Awards Engineering Contract for Legion Field to Global Learning Academy Sidewalks
legion-field-gla-sidewalks.d: The City Council selected a firm to provide construction management, engineering and inspection services for a new sidewalk corridor connecting Legion Field to Global Learning Academy. The project is intended to improve pedestrian safety on a school

cra-interlocal-amendment.h: Pensacola Extends Administrative Services Agreement Between City and Community Redevelopment Agency
cra-interlocal-amendment.d: An amendment to the 2016 interlocal agreement between Pensacola and its Community Redevelopment Agency governs how city departments provide staff support to the CRA. The council approved Amendment No. 2 on April 23.

architect-advisor-brian-spencer.h: Pensacola Appoints Architect Brian Spencer to CRA Urban Design Review Role
architect-advisor-brian-spencer.d: The City Council approved Brian Spencer as the primary architect advisor to the Community Redevelopment Agency's abbreviated design review process. The role supports faster turnaround on small-scale projects in the city's historic overlay districts.

reverb-cra-rejection-news.h: Pensacola CRA Rejects $58 Million Tax Rebate Application for Reverb Hard Rock Hotel at Maritime Park
reverb-cra-rejection-news.d: Community Redevelopment Agency Administrator Victoria D'Angelo cited six problems with the developer's 345-page application in an April 16 letter, including the project's failure to clear the "but-for" test required under Florida law. The next CRA bo

bay-center-189m-workshop.h: City and County Leaders Coalesce Around $189 Million Bay Center Plan in Joint Workshop
bay-center-189m-workshop.d: City and county elected officials spent two hours April 16 at the 41-year-old Pensacola Bay Center working through competing visions for a $189 million redevelopment. A three-part framework emerged: $71 million in arena renovations, a separate flexib

escambia-voucher-repayment.h: Escambia School Board Demands $3 Million Repayment From State Over Voucher Students Still in District Classrooms
escambia-voucher-repayment.d: The board voted April 22 to send a letter to the Florida Department of Education seeking reimbursement for 335 students who left the district under the state voucher program but later returned, and whom the district has educated this year without sta

escambia-schools-18m-shortfall.h: Escambia County Public Schools Face $18 Million Budget Gap as Five-Year Enrollment Decline Compounds
escambia-schools-18m-shortfall.d: The district has lost an average of 500 students per year over the past five years, driven by voucher expansion and demographic change. Per-pupil funding follows the student. The cumulative loss now translates to roughly $18 million missing from the

santa-rosa-sales-tax-ballot.h: Santa Rosa County Commissioners Approve Ballot Language for November Half-Cent Sales Tax Renewal
santa-rosa-sales-tax-ballot.d: Voters will be asked in November whether to extend the existing half-cent local option sales tax for another five years beginning January 2027. The tax generates roughly $16 million annually for road, bridge, public safety and parks projects. The cur

santa-rosa-farmland-protection.h: Santa Rosa County Adds Farmland Protection to Sales Tax Ballot Question Through New Agricultural Legacy Program
santa-rosa-farmland-protection.d: Commissioners voted in March to create the Agricultural Legacy Land Program, which would use sales tax revenue to fund conservation easements that pay landowners to keep farmland in agricultural use. The program is now folded into the November ballot

uwf-southern-illinois-opener.h: UWF Will Open Its Division I Football Era Against Southern Illinois on Aug. 27 at Pen Air Field
uwf-southern-illinois-opener.d: The Argonauts will open their first season as a Football Championship Subdivision program at home against Southern Illinois on Thursday, August 27 at Pen Air Field. UWF announced its move to NCAA Division I on April 2 and will play its first season i

ekos-warrington-affordable-housing.h: $41.9 Million Affordable Housing Development Breaks Ground in Warrington, Half the Units Reserved for Military Families
ekos-warrington-affordable-housing.d: A 120-unit affordable housing complex broke ground this month at 280 Dogwood Drive in Warrington, half its units reserved for active-duty military, veterans and their families. The $41.9 million project is the first Pensacola development for Miami-ba

pns-terminal-expansion-update.h: Pensacola International Airport's $152 Million Terminal Expansion Crosses Six-Month Construction Mark
pns-terminal-expansion-update.d: Six months into a two-year build, Pensacola International Airport's $152 million terminal expansion is on schedule. The project will add a five-gate concourse, expand security and parking, and lift the airport's gate count from 12 to 17 — capacity th

superintendent-ballot-referendum.h: Escambia County Voters Will Decide in August Whether to Return to Electing the School Superintendent
superintendent-ballot-referendum.d: The county commission voted 4-1 on April 1 to place the school board's request on the August 18 primary ballot, despite community pushback urging the question be moved to the higher-turnout November general election. If voters approve, the first elec

american-magic-saildrone-spectre.h: A Pensacola Sailing Team Is Now Building Weapons of War
american-magic-saildrone-spectre.d: American Magic and Saildrone unveiled the Spectre at Sea-Air-Space in April — a 170-foot autonomous warship for submarine hunting and missile strikes. Its propulsion wing is built at the Port of Pensacola.

american-magic-hpc-opening.h: American Magic Opens $20.8 Million High Performance Center at Port of Pensacola
american-magic-hpc-opening.d: American Magic, the New York Yacht Club's America's Cup challenger, opened its $20.8 million High Performance Center at the Port of Pensacola in January. City officials are calling it the largest single private capital investment at the port in more

mayors-race-2026-primer.h: Five Candidates Have Filed for the 2026 Pensacola Mayor's Race. Here's Where the Field Stands.
mayors-race-2026-primer.d: Five candidates have pre-filed for Pensacola mayor, setting up the most contested mayoral cycle since the 2022 election that put Mayor D.C. Reeves in office. The primary is August 18. If no one clears 50 percent, the top two finishers face a November

council-bcc-candidate-filings.h: Boyce White Files for City Council District 4; Joe Vinson Files for County Commission District 4 With No Party Affiliati
council-bcc-candidate-filings.d: Joe Vinson filed April 13 for Escambia County Commission District 4 with no party affiliation. Boyce White filed in January for City Council District 4. The County District 4 seat is currently held by Mike Kohler, who has not announced his re-electio

uwf-287g-student-walkout.h: UWF Students Walk Out Over University's Participation in 287(g) Immigration Enforcement Program
uwf-287g-student-walkout.d: Roughly 30 students walked out of class at the University of West Florida on April 14 to protest the university's participation in 287(g), a federal program that allows campus police to perform certain immigration enforcement duties under ICE supervi

jenkins-sentencing-pensacola-detective.h: Pensacola Man Sentenced to 20 Months in Federal Prison for Threatening Police Detective in Homicide Case
jenkins-sentencing-pensacola-detective.d: Tamal W. Jenkins, 20, was sentenced to 20 months in federal prison on April 14 after pleading guilty to transmitting a threatening communication with intent to extort. Federal prosecutors said Jenkins threatened a Pensacola Police Department detectiv

reeves-town-hall-may-june.h: Mayor Reeves Announces Spring and Summer Town Hall Schedule Across Pensacola Council Districts
reeves-town-hall-may-june.d: Mayor D.C. Reeves and city department directors will hold a series of community town halls in May and June, beginning in District 5 and rotating through each of the city's seven council districts. The format includes department updates and direct res

bay-center-spring-concerts-2026.h: Pensacola Bay Center Books Brandon Lake, Alice Cooper and Kevin Hart for Spring and Summer 2026
bay-center-spring-concerts-2026.d: The 41-year-old downtown arena's spring and summer schedule includes Christian artist Brandon Lake, rock veteran Alice Cooper and comedian Kevin Hart alongside the venue's anchor tenants. The lineup arrives as elected officials weigh a $189 million r

nas-pensacola-200th-birthday.h: NAS Pensacola's 200th Birthday Honored on U.S. House Floor by Rep. Jimmy Patronis
nas-pensacola-200th-birthday.d: Florida's 1st District congressman marked the installation's bicentennial in a speech to the House of Representatives on April 23, tracing the base's history from President John Quincy Adams' 1826 establishment to its current role training 60 percent

citadel-shield-solid-curtain-2026.h: NAS Pensacola Completes Two-Week Force Protection Exercise Testing Base Response to Simulated Threats
citadel-shield-solid-curtain-2026.d: Naval Air Station Pensacola spent two weeks in early April under simulated attack — armed intruders at the gate, mass casualties on the flight line, communications cut between the base and regional command — as part of Citadel Shield-Solid Curtain 20

blue-angels-2026-practice-season.h: Blue Angels' 2026 Practice Season Is in Full Swing Above NAS Pensacola
blue-angels-2026-practice-season.d: The Navy flight demonstration squadron is in regular practice flights over NAS Pensacola through October, with free public viewing at the National Naval Aviation Museum on most practice days. Base access for non-DoD visitors continues under the 2025

ice-flyers-playoff-run-ends.h: Ice Flyers' First Playoff Run in Four Years Ends in Overtime Game 3 Loss at Evansville
ice-flyers-playoff-run-ends.d: Pensacola fell 4-3 in overtime to the defending champion Evansville Thunderbolts on April 12, ending the team's deepest playoff run since winning the President's Cup in 2021. The Bay Center Whiteout for Game 2 drew 4,772 fans — the largest SPHL quart

sam-rhodes-sphl-rookie-year.h: Ice Flyers' Sam Rhodes Named SPHL Co-Rookie of the Year
sam-rhodes-sphl-rookie-year.d: The Pensacola forward led all SPHL rookies in points and power play goals during the 2025-26 regular season. The league announced the honor on April 24, with Rhodes sharing Co-Rookie of the Year with Macon Mayhem forward Khaden Henry.

blue-wahoos-sky-jockeys-debut.h: Blue Wahoos Debut 'Pensacola Sky Jockeys' Alternate Identity at Mid-April Homestand
blue-wahoos-sky-jockeys-debut.d: The Miami Marlins' Double-A affiliate wore its new Blue Angels-inspired alternate uniforms for the first time at an April 15 home game against the Biloxi Shuckers. The Sky Jockeys identity, unveiled in January at Pensacola International Airport, is a

uwf.h: UWF Is Going Division I. The City Should Pay Attention.

propertytax.h: Mayor Reeves Warns State Tax Plan Could Gut City Budget by $9M
propertytax.d: A proposed Florida property tax overhaul could cost Pensacola more than a quarter of its non-public safety operating budget if voters approve it.

escambia-childers.h: Escambia Commission and Clerk Childers Clash Over Funding Championship Basketball Team
escambia-childers.d: County commissioners and Clerk Pam Childers disagree on whether public funds can support a state champion girls basketball team headed to a national event.

warrington.h: Warrington's Damaged Hotel Gets a Second Look From New Owners
warrington.d: Escambia commissioners are set to vote on a redevelopment proposal for a hotel left in disrepair since Hurricane Sally in 2020.

gasprices.h: Gas Prices Are Hitting Pensacola Beach Boaters Where It Hurts
gasprices.d: Rising fuel costs tied to ongoing tensions with Iran are pushing marina prices to levels most recreational boaters haven't seen in years.

superintendent.h: Escambia Voters Will Decide in August: Elect or Appoint the School Superintendent?
superintendent.d: The Escambia primary ballot will carry a referendum on how the county selects its school superintendent.

blueangels.h: Flight Line Access Restored at Naval Aviation Museum After Brief Hold
blueangels.d: Access to the flight line behind the National Naval Aviation Museum for Blue Angels practice viewings was suspended March 31, then restored. Here's the current access situation and what visitors need to know.

wahoos.h: Blue Wahoos Complete Ballpark Upgrades Before Home Opener
wahoos.d: The Pensacola Blue Wahoos completed upgrades to Blue Wahoos Stadium at Community Maritime Park ahead of the team's 2026 home opener.

lost.h: $108 Million Later: What LOST IV Built for Pensacola
lost.d: The decade-long Local Option Sales Tax program wrapped in 2025 after generating more than $108 million for city projects. A breakdown of where the money went — and what comes next.

barrancas.h: Barrancas Avenue Lanes Reduce Starting Monday — Here's What to Know
barrancas.d: Barrancas Avenue between L Street and I Street will drop to two lanes beginning April 6. No end date has been given.

gulfbreeze.h: Gulf Breeze Soccer Complex: Costs Climbing, Questions Growing
gulfbreeze.d: Santa Rosa County asked for a contractor update on the Gulf Breeze soccer complex's progress and costs. The numbers aren't settled — and the project has a longer history of delays than most residents know.

op-uwf.h: Division I Is a Bet on the City, Not Just the University
op-uwf.d: The move to NCAA Division I isn't about football or scholarship money. It's about whether Pensacola is serious about competing for the next generation.

op-propertytax.h: The Property Tax Plan Is a State Power Grab Dressed Up as Voter Relief
op-propertytax.d: Tallahassee is pitching a ballot measure that would gut local budgets across Florida. Mayor Reeves is right to sound the alarm. Here's why the pitch doesn't hold up.

op-superintendent.h: The Superintendent Question Deserves More Than a Ballot Checkbox
op-superintendent.d: In August, Escambia voters weigh in on whether to elect or appoint the school superintendent. The structural choice matters more than most people realize.

crawfish.h: Pensacola Crawfish Festival Returns April 24 Downtown
crawfish.d: Fiesta Pensacola's annual crawfish festival is back at downtown's core with live entertainment, vendors and large-scale crawfish boils.

mullet-toss.h: Flora-Bama Mullet Toss: The Gulf Coast's Stranger, Better Tradition
mullet-toss.d: Every April, people line up on the state line to throw dead fish into Alabama. It works.

gaffigan.h: Jim Gaffigan Comes to the Bay Center April 17
gaffigan.d: The touring comedian brings his current run to Pensacola Bay Center this month.

easthill-traffic.h: East Hill's Street Grid Is Getting Its First Major Resurfacing in 15 Years
easthill-traffic.d: The city is queuing up a $4.2 million paving contract that covers more than a dozen streets between Cervantes and Gadsden.

escambia-budget-preview.h: Escambia County's Budget Season Starts Early — and the Numbers Are Already Hard
escambia-budget-preview.d: The county administrator flagged a projected $14 million structural gap at a workshop this week. What it means and who it will affect.

pensacola-water-system.h: Pensacola's Water System Passed Its Annual State Inspection. Here's What the Report Actually Says.
pensacola-water-system.d: The city water utility came back clean from its annual Florida DEP review. The report also identifies two aging pump stations that need capital attention within five years.

downtown-parking.h: The Downtown Parking Study Is Done. The City Hasn't Acted on It.
downtown-parking.d: A consultant delivered a 90-page parking management plan to city staff eight months ago. Here's what it recommends and why nothing has moved.

navy-federal-expansion.h: Navy Federal Confirms Third Campus Building in Beulah
navy-federal-expansion.d: The credit union's third office building in the Beulah corridor broke ground this week, with capacity for roughly 1,200 additional employees when fully staffed.

palafox-rezoning.h: Palafox Rezoning Request Would Allow 7-Story Mixed-Use at South Hill Edge
palafox-rezoning.d: A developer is asking the city to rezone a parcel on the north end of South Hill from C-1 to C-3. The project would bring housing and retail but changes the character of a transition block.

nas-housing.h: NAS Pensacola Family Housing Wait Times Are Back Up. The Base Blames Contractor Delays.
nas-housing.d: Average wait for on-base family housing is back above six months for E-5 and below, reversing a decline that had improved access through 2024.

blue-angels-schedule.h: Blue Angels Full 2026 Air Show Schedule Released — Pensacola Practice Season Begins in April
blue-angels-schedule.d: The team published its 35-show, 26-city schedule for 2026 this week. Local practice runs at Sherman Field start later this month.

uwf-track.h: UWF Track and Field Posts Best Regional Finish in Program History Heading Into D-I Transition Year
uwf-track.d: Three Argonauts qualified for NCAA Division II regionals this week. The program carries the momentum directly into its Division I transition.

pensacola-fc.h: Pensacola FC Opens Its Spring Season Saturday at Ashton Brosnaham
pensacola-fc.d: The local semi-pro club opens its 2026 NPSL campaign this weekend after three consecutive playoff appearances.

op-budget.h: Escambia's Budget Gap Needs a Public Conversation, Not a Quiet Fix
op-budget.d: The county faces a $14 million structural shortfall heading into FY2027. There's a right way and a wrong way to close it — and the public deserves to know which path the commission is taking.

op-nas-housing.h: The Housing Crunch at NAS Is a Community Problem, Not Just a Base Problem
op-nas-housing.d: When military families can't afford to live here, the problem reaches beyond the fence line. Pensacola should treat it that way.

bands-beach-guide.h: Your Complete Guide to Bands on the Beach 2026
bands-beach-guide.d: The summer concert series at Gulfside Pavilion opens April 7. Here's what you need to know about the lineup, logistics and the spots that fill up first.

pensacola-art-walk.h: Pensacola Art Walk Turns 20 This April — What's Changed and What Hasn't
pensacola-art-walk.d: Pensacola's monthly Gallery Night has been running on the last Friday of the month since 2006. A look at what it built and where it is now.

fiesta-pensacola.h: Fiesta Pensacola 2026: Everything on the Calendar Through Memorial Day
fiesta-pensacola.d: From the Crawfish Festival to the Cinco de Mayo celebration, Fiesta Pensacola runs the spring event season. A full guide to what's coming.

fricker-center.h: City Council Awards $7.8M Fricker Resource Center Renovation
fricker-center.d: The council approved the contract at its March 26 meeting. The largest public facility investment in the Brownsville corridor in years.

cdbg-buyout.h: City Accepts $1M Federal Grant for Voluntary Home Buyouts in Flood-Prone Areas
cdbg-buyout.d: A CDBG-DR grant through Florida Commerce funds voluntary purchases of flood-damaged homes. Participation is entirely the homeowner's choice.

garden-street-grocery.h: A Full-Service Grocery on Garden Street: Still Waiting, Still Needed
garden-street-grocery.d: Downtown Pensacola has been without a full-service grocery store for more than a decade. Multiple proposals. No store. It's time to ask why.

lakeview-demo.h: Lakeview Center's Former Campus Is Being Cleared. What Comes Next Isn't Settled.
lakeview-demo.d: The mental health nonprofit has vacated its long-held Brent Lane property near Bayou Texar. The site is being cleared with no public development plan announced.

beach-path.h: Escambia Approves $492K for Extended Pensacola Beach Multi-Use Path
beach-path.d: The March 26 county commission vote funds two phases that will eventually connect Via de Luna to north of the new toll gantry.

uwf-d1-schedule.h: UWF's First Division I Football Schedule Is Out. Here's Who They're Playing.
uwf-d1-schedule.d: The Argonauts released their 2026 UAC football schedule the same day as the D-I announcement. It is a real schedule against real programs.

psc-pirates.h: Pensacola State's Baseball Pirates Are in First Place. They Usually Are.
psc-pirates.d: The PSC Pirates lead the Panhandle Conference at midseason. A program that has been a national contender for a decade and still does not get enough local attention.

pso-100th.h: Pensacola Symphony's 100th Anniversary Season Closes April 25 With Dvořák's New World
pso-100th.d: The PSO's centennial season ends with its most ambitious program of the year. It's worth making time for.

symphony-100.h: The Orchestra That Survived Everything: PSO at 100
symphony-100.d: Pensacola has had an orchestra since 1926. It survived the Depression, two wars, a hurricane and a changing city. Here's what a century of music looks like from inside a mid-sized American port.

beach-art-wine.h: Pensacola Beach Art and Wine Festival Returns May 9
beach-art-wine.d: The Boardwalk turns into an outdoor gallery and tasting for a Saturday afternoon. One of the calmer events on the spring beach calendar.

disney-on-ice.h: Disney on Ice Brings Its Pensacola Run to the Bay Center May 9-10
disney-on-ice.d: Three shows over two days. If you have kids between 4 and 10, this is the most reliable family option on the spring calendar.

fiesta-parade.h: Grand Fiesta Parade Set for May 29 Down Palafox
fiesta-parade.d: The Fiesta of Five Flags parade closes out Pensacola's spring event season. DeLuna's landing has been re-enacted here every year since 1959.

santana-bay-center.h: Santana Brings the Oneness Tour to Pensacola Bay Center April 1
santana-bay-center.d: One of rock's most distinctive guitarists plays the Bay Center in what may be a final major touring run.

zz-top-saenger.h: ZZ Top at the Saenger Theatre, April 2
zz-top-saenger.d: The Texas trio plays the Saenger — one of the more intimate rooms for a band that has been filling arenas for fifty years.

gabriel-iglesias.h: Gabriel Iglesias Brings His 1976 Tour to the Bay Center April 11
gabriel-iglesias.d: Fluffy fills 9,000-seat arenas without breaking a sweat. This one should be no different.

mrs-doubtfire-saenger.h: Mrs. Doubtfire the Musical Opens a Two-Night Run at the Saenger April 7-8
mrs-doubtfire-saenger.d: The Broadway touring production of the 1993 film comes to Palafox Street for two nights.

gallery-night-april.h: Gallery Night April 17: Navy Days Theme, Modified Palafox Route
gallery-night-april.d: The monthly arts walk continues during Palafox construction — moved to Government and Jefferson. This month's theme is Vintage Vibes and Valor.

panchiko-vinyl.h: Panchiko at Vinyl Music Hall April 20
panchiko-vinyl.d: The British indie band with the unlikely cult following plays the 850-person room on South Palafox.

clutch-vinyl.h: Clutch at Vinyl Music Hall May 1
clutch-vinyl.d: The Maryland rock band — 35 years running, no radio hits, enormous following — headlines the 850-person venue on Palafox.

event.h: Alice Cooper at Pensacola Bay Center May 8
event.d: Fifty-plus years of theatrical rock. The show has always been the show.

pensacola-pride.h: Pensacola Pride Returns to Park East the Memorial Day Weekend
pensacola-pride.d: The annual beach celebration at Park East draws visitors from across the Southeast. Here is what to expect for 2026.

pickles-wings-festival.h: Pickles and Wings Family Festival Hits Seville Square May 16
pickles-wings-festival.d: Free entry, regional food vendors, live music and a setup that actually works for families. Mark the date.

krewe-sirens-beach.h: Krewe of Sirens Beach Day at Bounce Beach May 2
krewe-sirens-beach.d: The local krewe hosts an afternoon on the beach at the start of May. Low-key, community-focused, free to attend.

bavarian-circus.h: Germany's Great Bavarian Circus Sets Up at the Fairgrounds April 30 Through May 10
bavarian-circus.d: A touring European circus at the Pensacola Interstate Fairgrounds for eleven days. Worth knowing about if you have kids.

pensacola-comedy-club.h: Pensacola Comedy Club Runs Monthly at Genie's Coffee Shop
pensacola-comedy-club.d: A local comedy showcase at a coffee shop on Cervantes. Small room, local talent, worth supporting.

palafox-market.h: Palafox Market: Every Saturday Morning, Year-Round
palafox-market.d: The outdoor market at Plaza Ferdinand runs every Saturday, rain or shine. Fresh produce, local baked goods, art and plants from vendors who grew or made what they're selling.

iration-vinyl.h: Iration at Vinyl Music Hall May 19
iration-vinyl.d: The Hawaii-based reggae-rock band plays the Palafox Street venue on a Tuesday night.

wahoos-opening-home.h: Blue Wahoos Home Opener Is April 3. Here's Why the Stadium Is Worth the Night.
wahoos-opening-home.d: The Wahoos open their 2026 home schedule Friday at Blue Wahoos Stadium on the bay. The park is among the best small-market venues in the country.

escambia-schools-budget.h: Escambia Schools Face $14M Budget Gap Heading Into FY2027 Planning
escambia-schools-budget.d: Rising personnel costs and flat state funding have the district looking at a structural shortfall.

uwf-enrollment-growth.h: UWF Enrollment Surpasses 15,600 and the D-I Transition Is Just Getting Started
uwf-enrollment-growth.d: The university has grown steadily for five consecutive years. Division I will accelerate the trajectory.

psc-dual-enrollment.h: PSC Dual Enrollment Is Growing. The Access Gap Is Not Closing.
psc-dual-enrollment.d: The college served 2,800 high school students last year. Participation rates across the county are deeply uneven.

escambia-teacher-shortage.h: Escambia Schools Still Has 87 Teacher Vacancies. The Pipeline Problem Is Structural.
escambia-teacher-shortage.d: The district is filling the easy positions. The ones that remain are the hard core — special ed, math, secondary science.

op-beach-nourishment.h: The Beach Nourishment Worked. The Follow-Through Can't Wait Another Decade.
op-beach-nourishment.d: Pensacola Beach just finished a $35 million sand project. That investment now needs a maintenance plan — not another 10-year gap before the next cycle.

op-lakeview.h: What Happens at the Old Lakeview Campus Will Define That Block for 50 Years
op-lakeview.d: The site is being cleared and nobody is saying publicly what comes next. That silence is a problem.

op-ecua-water.h: Pensacola's Water Infrastructure Is Fine — Until It Isn't
op-ecua-water.d: The city passed its annual water inspection. Two pump stations are flagged for future replacement. The time to fund them is now, not when they fail in a storm.

op-bayou-chico.h: Bayou Chico Has Been Ignored Long Enough
op-bayou-chico.d: Pensacola has three major bayous. Bayou Texar gets the waterfront homes. Bayou Grande gets the marina traffic. Bayou Chico gets the neglect.

op-gateway.h: The Gateway Project Is a Good Investment. Make Sure It Stays One.
op-gateway.d: The Bob Sikes Toll Plaza replacement is the right call. The execution details will determine whether it actually solves the problem.

op-affordable-housing.h: Pensacola Is Growing. Its Housing Policy Is Not.
op-affordable-housing.d: The city is adding residents, UWF is about to expand its profile, and Navy Federal is building again. Housing supply hasn't kept up and the policy response has been minimal.

history-galvez.h: Yo Solo: The 1781 Battle That Gave Pensacola to Spain and Helped End the Revolution
history-galvez.d: In March 1781, a Spanish general did something his own naval commanders refused to do. He sailed alone into a defended bay. What happened next changed the course of the Revolutionary War.

history-naval-aviation.h: The Cradle of Naval Aviation: How Pensacola Got That Name and Why It Still Fits
history-naval-aviation.d: In 1914, the Navy sent seven men to Pensacola with a handful of aircraft. What they built over the next 110 years became the most important aviation training complex in American military history.

public-pay.h: In the Sunshine: What Escambia County and Pensacola's Elected Officials Actually Earn
public-pay.d: Florida law says every government salary is public record. But knowing where to look — and what it all means — is another story. This is the first installment of The Flightline's Let the Sunshine In, dedicated to the transparency legacy Pensacola's o

superintendent-ballot.h: Commission Votes 4-1 to Put Superintendent Referendum on August Primary Ballot
superintendent-ballot.d: The Escambia County Commission approved placing the elected-vs.-appointed superintendent question on the August 18 primary ballot — over objections that the primary's lower turnout will skew the outcome.

i10-expansion.h: I-10 Widening to Six Lanes: The $236M Project Reshaping Pensacola's Main Artery
i10-expansion.d: FDOT is widening I-10 through Escambia County to six lanes and rebuilding the Highway 29 interchange. Sixty-two thousand drivers use it every day. Here's what the project covers, what it costs, and what changes when it's done.

garden-street-construction.h: One Year In: West Garden Street's Businesses Are Still Open — But the Fight Is Real
garden-street-construction.d: FDOT's $22 million Garden Street reconstruction project entered its second year in April 2026. The road will be better when it's done in mid-2027. But the businesses on it are dealing with the reality of construction now.

palafox-reconstruction.h: The New Palafox: What's Actually Getting Built and When It Finishes
palafox-reconstruction.d: The City of Pensacola's Palafox Street stormwater and ADA project started January 5, 2026. Completion targets run through late 2026 in phases. Here's what the work covers and what changes when it's done.

east-hill-demolition.h: Fairnie Hall: The 1934 Home at 1182 E. Lakeview Ave. Is Approved for Demolition
east-hill-demolition.d: The City of Pensacola's Architectural Review Board reviewed the Fairnie Hill home in February 2026 and found it historically significant — but that designation only delays demolition. It cannot stop it.

gulf-breeze-cra.h: Gulf Breeze Is Rebuilding Its Commercial Core. The CRA Has a 393-Acre Mandate and a 37-Year Clock.
gulf-breeze-cra.d: The Gulf Breeze Community Redevelopment Agency has been targeting the US 98 corridor since 1989. After years of incremental moves, the agency is in an active period on parking reform, redevelopment proposals, and downtown vacancy.

florida-dei-bill.h: Florida's 2026 Session Is Over. Here's What Passed That Affects Pensacola.
florida-dei-bill.d: The Florida Legislature adjourned March 13 without passing a budget. Special sessions for redistricting, property tax, and the budget are expected in April. Several bills that did pass will directly affect Pensacola and Escambia County.

tpo-i10-work-program.h: I-10 Is Getting Six Lanes. The TPO Just Approved the Work Program That Funds It.
tpo-i10-work-program.d: The Florida-Alabama Transportation Planning Organization accepted FDOT's FY2027-2031 Tentative Work Program at its February meeting, locking in the I-10 widening and a series of ITS fiber and interchange projects across Escambia and Santa Rosa counti

warrior-sacrifice-way.h: Navy Blvd Could Be Renamed to Honor 2019 NAS Pensacola Attack Victims
warrior-sacrifice-way.d: SB 628, filed by Sen. Don Gaetz, would designate a stretch of Navy Boulevard in Escambia County as 'Warrior Sacrifice Way' — honoring the three sailors killed in the December 2019 terrorist attack at Naval Air Station Pensacola.

tpo-hayne-street.h: TPO Votes to Restructure $918K Hayne Street Project, Cutting Scope by Nearly Half
tpo-hayne-street.d: The Florida-Alabama Transportation Planning Organization unanimously amended its Transportation Improvement Program in February, changing the North Hayne Street project limits and reducing the roadwork corridor from 0.459 miles to 0.27 miles.

sorrento-road-safety.h: The TPO Is Studying Sorrento Road. A Safety Review Is Underway Between Navy Blvd and Gulf Beach Highway.
sorrento-road-safety.d: FDOT Traffic Operations launched an interim safety study on the Sorrento Road corridor after the Florida-Alabama TPO requested it at its August 2025 meeting. Preliminary results were presented to the board in February.

florida-special-session.h: Florida's Legislature Left Without a Budget. Gaetz Says the Special Session Will Deliver on Property Taxes.
florida-special-session.d: The 2026 regular session ended March 13 without a budget or a property tax deal. A special session is set for the week of April 20. Sen. Don Gaetz says DeSantis will have a plan and the Senate will pass it. The Senate chair of Appropriations says the

dei-ban-local-impact.h: Florida's DEI Ban Is Now Law. Here's What It Means for Pensacola and Escambia.
dei-ban-local-impact.d: SB 1134, passed in the 2026 legislative session, prohibits Florida cities, counties and other local governments from funding, carrying out or promoting diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives. Local officials face removal. Governments face lawsui

gulf-breeze-council-parking.h: Gulf Breeze Approves 8-Year Parking Initiative for US 98 Corridor
gulf-breeze-council-parking.d: The Gulf Breeze City Council has approved an 8-year, grant-funded parking program targeting its primary commercial corridor along US 98. The CRA Board meets Monday, April 6, to advance additional redevelopment work along the strip.

escambia-school-board-calendar.h: Escambia School Board Heads Into Spring With Talent Shortages, a Voucher Audit and a Superintendent Ballot Question
escambia-school-board-calendar.d: Three issues are converging at the Escambia County School District this spring: a national teacher shortage affecting local classrooms, a state audit documenting accountability failures in the voucher program that drains district enrollment, and an A

florida-special-session-april.h: Florida Is Heading Into Special Session. Pensacola Has a Lot Riding on What Comes Out.
florida-special-session-april.d: The 2026 regular legislative session ended March 13 without a state budget or a property tax deal. Governor DeSantis has called a special session for the week of April 20 to resolve all three outstanding issues. Sen. Don Gaetz says the governor will

escambia-school-board-capital.h: Escambia School Board Approves Capital Projects — Tate High Gets $4.1M Gym Renovation
escambia-school-board-capital.d: The School Board of Escambia County approved a series of capital construction and maintenance projects at its recent meeting. The largest single item: a $4.1 million gymnasium renovation at Tate High School in Cantonment.

wfrpc-regional-planning.h: West Florida Regional Planning Council Focuses on Housing, Infrastructure and Resilience Across Six Counties
wfrpc-regional-planning.d: The West Florida Regional Planning Council serves as the state-designated regional planning body for a six-county area that includes Escambia and Santa Rosa. Its current work program covers housing affordability, coastal resilience and hurricane reco

gulf-breeze-council-march26.h: Gulf Breeze City Council: Development Review Board Active, Fire Board Meets March 19
gulf-breeze-council-march26.d: Gulf Breeze's elected government and advisory boards have had a full agenda through early 2026. The Development Review Board handles construction applications. The Fire Board is focused on volunteer recruitment. The CRA Board meets April 6.

hurricane-prep.h: Hurricane Season Is Coming. Here Is What Pensacola Families Should Do Now.
hurricane-prep.d: The Atlantic hurricane season opens June 1. Gulf Coast residents have a short window before storm activity picks up. A prep checklist sourced from Escambia County Emergency Management and state guidance.

20260406-fricker-renovation.h: Fricker Resource Center to Close for $10M Overhaul. Here's the Timeline.
20260406-fricker-renovation.d: City Council awarded the construction contract March 26. After-school and senior programs move to alternate sites. The rebuilt facility reopens June 2027.

20260406-nas-weekday-closure.h: NAS Pensacola Is Closed to the Public on Weekdays. What That Means for Visitors.
20260406-nas-weekday-closure.d: The base restricted civilian access to weekends only starting March 23. Everyone 18 and older now needs a background check before entering.

20260406-blue-angels-schedule.h: Blue Angels 2026 Practice Schedule Is Out. Here Are the Dates — and the Catches.
20260406-blue-angels-schedule.d: The team flies over Pensacola from April through October. But flight line viewing at the museum is suspended and the base is weekend-only.

20260406-project-maeve.h: A $76M Shipbuilding Facility Could Come to the Port of Pensacola. Here's Where It Stands.
20260406-project-maeve.d: Triumph Gulf Coast gave initial approval in January to Project Maeve — bringing shipbuilder Birdon America to the city-owned port. A final grant agreement is still pending.

20260406-easter-beach-tourism.h: Pensacola Beach Had 18 Hotel Rooms Left on Easter Weekend. The Season Is Already Here.
20260406-easter-beach-tourism.d: Gas hit $4 a gallon and airfares climbed, but visitors still packed the beach. Businesses reported summer-level crowds as early as spring break.

20260406-tunnel-road-closure.h: Tunnel Road Closes at the Bridge for Repairs Through Friday
20260406-tunnel-road-closure.d: Santa Rosa County says the week-long closure wraps April 10 weather permitting. Residents west of the bridge detour via Quintette and Curtis roads.

20260406-chumuckla-highway.h: Chumuckla Highway Has a Lane Shift at Giddens Road. Expect Minor Delays.
20260406-chumuckla-highway.d: Contractors moved the northbound right lane to the center on March 31 as part of a new turn lane project at the Giddens Lane intersection.

20260406-escambia-roads.h: Escambia County Is Repaving 47 Roads This Year. Here's Where the Money Is Going.
20260406-escambia-roads.d: The commission awarded an $8M contract to Roads Inc. of NWF in February, advancing 2027 funds to cover high-traffic routes across all five districts.

20260406-beach-blvd-lane.h: Pensacola Beach Boulevard Lane Closure Extended Through Friday
20260406-beach-blvd-lane.d: Work on the new toll plaza has kept the inside northbound exit lane closed since March 23. It should reopen Friday evening, April 10.

20260406-fish-house-merger.h: Atlas Oyster House Is Gone. The Fish House Absorbed It — and Got Bigger.
20260406-fish-house-merger.d: Great Southern Restaurants merged its two waterfront properties on April 1, creating a single expanded Fish House with over 300 feet of Pensacola Bay frontage.

20260406-restaurants.h: Nine New Restaurants Opened in the Pensacola Area in March
20260406-restaurants.d: From a coastal Mexican spot on the beach to the first Florida location of a Southern chain, last month brought a sustained wave of new dining.

20260406-beach-vitex.h: An Invasive Plant From Asia Is Spreading Across Pensacola Beach. UWF Wants Help Pulling It.
20260406-beach-vitex.d: Beach vitex has spread to 69 sites on Pensacola Beach since 2012. The annual April Weed Wrangle targets public land — but 47 of the 84 non-park sites are on private property.

20260406-drought.h: All of Escambia County Is Now in Severe Drought
20260406-drought.d: The U.S. Drought Monitor shows the entire county under severe drought, with extreme drought in the eastern portion. A chance of rain Sunday could provide some relief.

20260406-wahoos-skyjockeys.h: The Blue Wahoos Have a New Alternate Identity That Pays Tribute to Naval Aviation
20260406-wahoos-skyjockeys.d: The Pensacola Sky Jockeys debuted this season with a blue and gold color scheme borrowed directly from the Blue Angels. It's the team's first military-themed alternate brand.

20260406-wahoos-tv.h: Blue Wahoos Games Are Back on Local TV — With a New Weekly Show
20260406-wahoos-tv.d: All 69 home games return to local television on Blab TV and YurView Florida. A new behind-the-scenes program called Wahoos Weekly debuts Fridays at 5:30 p.m.

20260406-wahoos-rickwood.h: The Blue Wahoos Play at Rickwood Field on May 27. Here's Why That Matters.
20260406-wahoos-rickwood.d: Rickwood Field in Birmingham is the oldest professional ballpark in America, built in 1910. It will be the Wahoos' first appearance there.

20260406-uwf-artifacts.h: UWF Found Pre-Columbian Native American Artifacts on Campus While Prepping for a New Stadium
20260406-uwf-artifacts.d: An archaeological survey required before Darrell Gooden Stadium construction uncovered ceramics from two distinct Native American cultures — the oldest possibly dating to A.D. 600.

20260406-bay-center.h: The City Is Reviewing What to Do With the Bay Center
20260406-bay-center.d: The Community Redevelopment Agency took up a draft convention center analysis at its April 6 meeting — the latest sign the future of the Pensacola Bay Center is an active policy question.

20260406-alcaniz-streetscape.h: Alcaniz Street Is Getting a Streetscape Overhaul From Garden Street to the Bayfront
20260406-alcaniz-streetscape.d: The CRA approved plans April 6 for improvements along Alcaniz Street, a downtown corridor connecting the historic district to Bayfront Parkway.

20260406-uscgc-eagle.h: The USCGC Eagle Is Coming to Pensacola This Spring. The City Waived Its Docking Fee.
20260406-uscgc-eagle.d: The Coast Guard's 295-foot training barque — in service since 1946 — will berth at Plaza de Luna in May. The CRA voted Monday to waive docking fees.

20260406-city-county-joint.h: City Council and County Commission Are Meeting Together on April 16. That's Unusual.
20260406-city-county-joint.d: A formal joint session between the two governing bodies is rare. It is scheduled at the Bay Center — the same week the CRA is reviewing a convention center analysis.

20260406-affordable-housing.h: Pensacola Is Launching a Small-Scale Affordable Rental Housing Pilot
20260406-affordable-housing.d: The CRA approved a new program policy and issued an RFQ Monday for a pilot that would support small affordable rental developments in the city's redevelopment districts.

20260406-community-health-land.h: The City Is Donating Land on West Avery Street for a Community Health Facility
20260406-community-health-land.d: City Council votes Thursday to give 801 West Avery Street to Community Health Northwest Florida Foundation — removing it from the redevelopment area and transferring it for a health clinic.

port-birdon-project-maeve.h: Birdon America Is Building a $275M Shipyard at the Port of Pensacola, Bringing Up to 2,000 Jobs to the Region
port-birdon-project-maeve.d: The Triumph Gulf Coast board approved an initial $76 million grant for the project in January, clearing the way for a 400,000-square-foot naval manufacturing facility that would become the company's Southeastern U.S. headquarters.

nas-dod-personal-firearms-policy.h: Pentagon Ends 30-Year Personal Firearms Ban at Military Bases, Directly Citing the 2019 NAS Pensacola Terror Attack
nas-dod-personal-firearms-policy.d: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth signed the directive April 2, ordering base commanders to treat personal carry requests from service members with a presumption of approval — a policy that applies to NAS Pensacola, Whiting Field, Eglin and Hurlburt.

801-avery-community-health-clinic.h: Pensacola Is Donating City-Owned Land in West Pensacola to Bring Medical and Dental Care to the Neighborhood
801-avery-community-health-clinic.d: The Pensacola City Council votes Thursday on three linked agenda items that would transfer 801 W. Avery Street to Community Health Northwest Florida Foundation, the nonprofit arm of the region's largest federally qualified health center operator.

childers-discretionary-lawsuit-042426.h: Two Escambia nonprofits sue Clerk Pam Childers after she blocks $7,000 in commission-approved payments
childers-discretionary-lawsuit-042426.d: First Tee Gulf Coast and the Warrington Emergency Aid Center filed suit Friday in Escambia Circuit Court after Childers refused to release commission-approved funds.

escambia-student-code-conduct-042226.h: Escambia school board adopts new student conduct rules for 2026-27, adding drug screens for student drivers
escambia-student-code-conduct-042226.d: The board approved revised codes of conduct Tuesday. High schoolers who park on campus must consent to random drug screening, and blankets are banned from classrooms.

keyla-richardson-idol-homecoming-042326.h: Pensacola plans free hometown concert for Keyla Richardson if she makes American Idol Top 3
keyla-richardson-idol-homecoming-042326.d: Mayor Reeves is coordinating with Commissioner Lumon May on a free event at Community Maritime Park and Wahoos Stadium tentatively set for May 6.
